step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the agent1’s assessment. Substituting the hydroxyl ion with fluoride results in stronger bonds because it enhances crystalline stability through additional chemical interactions not present in pure hydroxyapatite. This makes fluoroapatite less susceptible to demineralization processes, effectively increasing its mechanical strength. Thus, my answer remains consistent with Option A.
